name: Poha Jalebi
shortDescription: Malwa's classic light savory breakfast paired with warm jalebi.
longDescription: A popular morning combination across Ujjain and Indore. Choose a clean, busy outlet and keep portions light before temple queues.

name: Dal Bafla
shortDescription: A hearty Madhya Pradesh meal served with dal, ghee, chutney and churma.
longDescription: Best planned as a relaxed lunch rather than before darshan or a long road transfer. Ask for lighter ghee portions when needed.

name: Bhutte Ka Kees
shortDescription: Grated corn cooked with milk, mustard and gentle Malwa spices.
longDescription: A distinctive regional snack commonly explored in Indore. Confirm spice level and ingredients for children or dietary restrictions.

name: Garadu
shortDescription: A crisp, spiced winter street-food specialty strongly associated with Indore.
longDescription: Enjoy a small shared portion at a clean, busy stall. It can be spicy and heavy, so it may not suit everyone before travel or darshan.
